label: Uncomplicated acute otitis media without mastoiditis concern
inclusion_criteria: Adult patient with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of acute otitis media, otitis media, or middle ear infection.
Clinician-entered treatment plan is present for pain control, antibiotics, delayed antibiotics, watchful waiting, or other medication instructions.

No mastoiditis, deep ear infection, eardrum perforation, tympanostomy tube, ear trauma, foreign body, otitis externa pathway, acute hearing loss, facial weakness, vertigo, severe systemic illness, meningitis, venous sinus thrombosis concern, or unstable vital signs documented.
No pediatric, recurrent/chronic, immunocompromised-host, unreliable watchful-waiting follow-up, or specialist-directed ENT pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Mastoiditis, deep ear infection, skull base infection, meningitis, venous sinus thrombosis, or severe systemic illness concern.
Perforated eardrum, tympanostomy tube, ear trauma, ear foreign body, acute hearing loss, vertigo, facial weakness, or specialist-directed ENT plan.
Otitis externa or ear canal infection pathway.
Immunocompromised host, recurrent or chronic ear infection, or pediatric pathway requiring individualized instructions.
default_follow_up: If your clinician prescribed antibiotics, follow up in 7 to 10 days or sooner if symptoms are not improving. If your clinician gave a watchful-waiting plan, follow up in 2 to 3 days if symptoms are not improving. Follow any different schedule your clinician gave you.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus and CDC support acute otitis media/middle-ear infection framing, ear pain and fever symptom education, clinician-directed observation or antibiotic decisions, and escalation for severe or worsening illness. These sources do not independently support ED-specific risk stratification, antibiotic selection, pediatric dosing, or follow-up timing, so runtime must require adult clinician diagnosis, clinician-entered treatment instructions, and structured absence of ear red flags.

terms: adult discharge diagnosis acute otitis media
adult patient with acute otitis media
adult diagnosed with acute otitis media
no perforation tube trauma foreign body hearing loss facial weakness vertigo
no mastoiditis perforation tube trauma foreign body hearing loss facial weakness vertigo meningitis concern

high_confidence_terms: adult discharge diagnosis acute otitis media

output_modifiers.label: Frail elderly follow-up modifier
output_modifiers.note: Shorten follow-up to 48 to 72 hours and confirm explicit return instructions.

output_modifiers.label: Diabetes follow-up modifier
output_modifiers.note: Confirm clinician-entered follow-up and return instructions because infection recovery may be less predictable.

review.notes: Phase 222 clinician feedback applied. Approved for narrow ontology mode after pathway-specific follow-up, frail-elderly output modifier, diabetes output modifier, and watchful-waiting follow-up-access blocker were reviewed.
